name: rasa.tenant.core
description: The foundational tenant brain/template installed into every tenant by default (the renamed rasa.orchestrator.core). Also carries the substrate-authoring tooling absorbed from the retired rasa.orchestrator.workspace: new-element, ship-element, refresh-registry, scan-drift, audit-substrate, lock-sequence, audit-canon-tasks.

rasa.note: Canonical tenant template (folded from rasa.orchestrator.core per canon SA-023 — orchestrator kind removed; the tenant IS the orchestrator) per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md v1.3+ §4 (required files) + §7 (install policies). v1.0.0 is the locked-down shape: forks may pin to this version and absorb later minor bumps as additive. shape_pattern='agnostic' here flags that this template does not commit to domain-shaped or structure-shaped; forks set their own. See content/SHAPE.md for the choice. Paired with rasa.domain.core v1.0.0 which serves the same role for the domain kind.

identity.one_liner: the foundational RasaOS tenant brain — the orchestrator a tenant installs by default
identity.subject: being the tenant (the orchestrator): installing + coordinating modules and registering domains
identity.goal: bring being the tenant (the orchestrator): installing + coordinating modules and registering domains into a project as an installable RasaOS tenant

element.description: Files mirrored into the install target per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. v1.0.0 ships all of these as opt-in scaffold — registered for check-manifest coverage but not installed automatically. Forks change policies as they populate content/, OR delete entries entirely for structure-shaped orchestrators that don't install at all.

files.note: Fork-time guide explaining domain-shaped vs structure-shaped orchestrator patterns. Not installed into consumer projects. Forks should delete this from their copy and replace with content/README.md describing what's actually shipped.

Orchestration skill scaffold. Ships the generic /sync + /promote skills (v1.3.0) — smart-pull / smart-push that operate against the project's persistent kit/<element>/ clone that the /kit-aware bin/init drops in at install. Both are domain-agnostic: they reference only the Element contract (lockfile, pinned_sha, overrides, install policies, the /kit stash), zero subject knowledge. Forks adopting this scaffold populate + change policy to directory-mirror. Structure-shaped tenants that don't install may delete this entry + the directory.
Orchestration rules scaffold. Forks adopting populate + change policy. Canonical install target is .claude/ root (rules install flat per domain-code precedent).
Claude subagent definitions scaffold. Forks adopting populate + change policy to directory-mirror.
Authoring roadmap (candidate skills/rules/agents for the substrate-authoring tooling) absorbed from the retired rasa.orchestrator.workspace per canon SA-023. Not installed into consumer projects.
Generic /whoami — states the composed RasaOS identity (canon SA-025).

seed.description: One-time files copied at install only. Per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. OPTIONAL for orchestrators (vs typical-required for domains). Structure-shaped orchestrators that don't install via bin/init can ignore or delete the seed/ folder entirely. The template ships both canonical templates so forks have starting material if they DO install.

files.note: Per-project Claude session contract. Lives in install-target root; project-owned forever. Placeholder-free (Element identity lives in .claude/rasa.lock.json — current install-policy taxonomy doesn't substitute placeholders in skip-if-exists files; flagged in domain-core v0.1.0 AUDIT).

Stamped with Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6. Substitutes {{ELEMENT_NAME}}, {{ELEMENT_KIND}}, {{ELEMENT_VERSION}}, {{ELEMENT_REPO}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init's init-only-with-sha handler.

The DEPLOYMENT identity layer (SA-025) — project-owned; what this project is, for whom, on what platform. bin/init generates the element layer (.claude/rasa-identity.md) from rasa.identity every install.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init. v1.0.0 ships none — forks add as needed.
